content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886
September 22, 2006

Stacey Fulhorst
Executive Director, Ethics Commission
City of San Diego
1010 Second Avenue, Suite 1530
San Diego, CA 92101

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-123

Dear Ms. Fulhorst:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”)1 concerning payments made by political parties for member communications. Because your request presents issues requiring a policy interpretation best made through a Commission Opinion under Government Code section 93114(a) or by adoption of a regulation pursuant to Government Code section 83112, we are declining to provide written advice on this matter. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(E).) Accordingly, your request has been withdrawn.

If you would like the Commission to consider the issues you have raised, please resubmit your request in the form of a request for opinion under section 83114(a) and regulation 18320. Your request should be submitted to the Executive Director, Mark Krause, with a copy provided to our General Counsel, Luisa Menchaca. Additionally, please provide copies of your request to representatives of the political parties involved:

Chuck Bell
Bell McAndrews & Hiltachk
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 801
Sacramento, CA 95814

Lance Olson
Olson Hagel & Fishburn LLP
555 Capitol Mall, Suite1425
Sacramento, CA 95814-4602

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: William J. Lenkeit
Senior Counsel
Legal Division

WJL:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-123
cc: Lance Olson
Chuck Bell
